name: disposing a value-less owns edge is swallowed by the idempotency refresh
description: A model that emits owns as a bare object edge (no value_json, object_entity_ref=the thing) must be able to end ownership. The sell note re-uses the SAME object entity with assertion=negated. Previously supersession.values_equal returned True on object_entity_id alone, so decide() took the re-extraction idempotency branch and the refresh path (which never writes assertion) swallowed the disposal, leaving one active asserted row with a 'no longer own' statement. Spec-correct (docs/ANALYSIS.md 'Assertion status': the wiki demotes everything below asserted; a negated owns is not an owned fact): the head must be negated/closed. Fixed: values_equal returns False on a flipped assertion, so the candidate falls through to per-kind newest-wins supersession -- the old head is superseded and the new head carries assertion=negated.

facts.statement: I no longer own the Civic.
